coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: You're 72 days out in taper with a 81/100 recovery score and 8+ hours of sleep—you're primed. Today is Wednesday; normally vert work, but you've already banked 57 miles last week and only 7.3 this week. Do a controlled 8–10 mile hill repeats session: 2mi easy, then 5×800m hill at 85–88% max HR (around 155–160 bpm based on your recent 136–139 bpm work), 2min recovery jog between reps, 2mi easy out. This maintains your alpine leg strength and neuromuscular sharpness without adding volume junk. Your recent long run was 20mi; you don't need to chase mileage now—you need precision.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: With hill repeats at 85–88% intensity, you'll burn glycogen hard. Eat a carb-forward meal 2–3 hours before the run (50–60g carbs: oats, banana, toast). During the 8–10 miler take 20–30g carbs if it runs past 75 minutes (sports drink or gel). Post-run within 30min: 20g protein + 60–80g carbs to restock glycogen and prime adaptation. Your 7.11h average sleep and today's 8.08h is excellent—don't undereat now or you'll blunt recovery before taper week finishes.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your resting HR is steady at 60 bpm and recovery score is high; you're not carrying fatigue. Sleep has been strong (8+ hours last night). Post-run, do 10–15min of easy walking or 200m strides to flush lactate, then ice bath or cold plunge if you have access (2–3min, 50–59°F). No aggressive strength work today—save that for non-running days. Your nervous system needs to consolidate this vert stimulus without cumulative load.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're in taper with 10 weeks to Julian Alps; the hardest training is behind you. Today's hill repeats serve two purposes: they remind your legs what race pace feels like on vert, and they confirm you're still sharp. Don't chase numbers or feel defensive about cutting volume—the 50K will test your aerobic base (built in Build 1–2) and your mental toughness in the final 10 miles. Use these hill reps to build confidence that your engine still responds. Recovery week is next; trust the plan.
